profile.name: BEMELINGUE Wilfried
profile.title: Computer Engineering Student specialized in AI, Deep Learning, and Machine Learning
profile.summary: Currently seeking a 6-month end-of-studies internship in the fields of Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, or Machine Learning. Based in Île-de-France, France. I speak French and fluent in English (TOEIC in progress).

experiences.title: Intern – Data Engineer (DataFactory Team)
experiences.description:
Intern in the DataFactory team at COMUTITRES (Île-de-France Mobilités). I architected and migrated legacy ETL data pipelines to Apache Airflow on AWS, significantly improving processing efficiency.

Key achievements:
• Engineered and migrated Python/SQL scripts to Airflow/AWS environment
• Developed automated retry mechanisms using JSON configurations to enhance pipeline reliability
• Designed and deployed KPI dashboards to support Business Intelligence decision-making
• Optimized SQL queries and Python processing scripts
• Strengthened CI/CD pipelines using GitLab for automated deployment

experiences.title: Intern – Logistics Tracking App Development
experiences.description:
Spearheaded the development and migration of a logistics tracking web application for air cargo.

Key achievements:
• Led the database migration from InterSystems Caché to InterSystems IRIS
• Developed a full-stack solution with a JavaScript front-end and ObjectScript/Python back-end
• Designed and integrated REST APIs for real-time air freight tracking
• Administered and configured the InterSystems IRIS server
• Authored comprehensive technical documentation for internal teams and clients

experiences.title: Intern – API Interface Development
experiences.description: Participated in the creation of a web application displaying air freight flows, connected to a REST API. Dynamic data integration using JavaScript, interface readability improvement, functional testing, and contribution to technical documentation.

experiences.title: Intern
experiences.description: Arduino and mechatronics training, creation of a Smart Parking system and a remote-controlled car.

education.degree: Major in Smart Computing for Artificial Graphs (4th and 5th years, final year in progress, CTI accredited)
education.details: EPITA is ranked among the top French engineering schools specializing in computer science and AI (Top 5 in IT-focused schools, CTI accredited). The SCIA‑G (Smart Computing for Artificial Graphs) major is one of its most advanced specializations, training engineers to design intelligent architectures on graph-structured data with a strong research and industry focus. The curriculum covers: graph neural networks (GNN, Graph Transformer, GCN, GAT), explainable AI (XAI), reinforcement learning (DQN, PPO, A3C), quantitative finance (stochastic modeling, Wiener processes, Black-Scholes), medical and biological data processing, MLOps (Docker, CI/CD, model deployment), convex and non-convex optimization, and natural language processing (NLP/LLMs). The pedagogy relies on research projects, competitive challenges, and real-world industrial use cases.

education.degree: Master’s in Computer Engineering (3rd year, CTI accredited)
education.details: EPITA is ranked among the top French engineering schools specialized in computer science (Top 5, CTI accredited). The 3rd year forms the core common curriculum before specialization, and its reputation is largely built on the infamous 'piscines' — intensive 3 to 5-day deep dives where students are literally immersed in code, often coding late into the night. During a piscine, a single theme (algorithmics, C systems programming, networking, compilation, databases) is worked on continuously, with progressive exercises, mini-projects due each evening, and constant collective pressure. There is no room for distraction: logic, syntactic precision, and stress management are tested simultaneously. On top of these, 'rush' projects push teams to deliver a functional piece of software in 48 to 72 hours. This year builds rare technical resilience, an intense relationship with code, and the ability to perform in high-demand professional environments.

ECE Paris is a generalist engineering school founded in 1919, ranked among the top 20 French post-bac schools (L'Étudiant ranking) and CTI accredited. Its RNCP Bachelor (Level 6) prepares students for careers in digital technology and engineering. In the 2nd year, the curriculum covers: object-oriented programming (Java, Python), algorithms and data structures, relational databases (SQL), networking and protocols (TCP/IP, HTTP), web development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript), applied mathematics (linear algebra, calculus, probability), and an introduction to embedded systems. Courses are built around multidisciplinary team projects that develop project management skills and professional application of technical knowledge.

EPSI (École Professionnelle des Sciences Informatiques) is a school specializing in digital technology, offering RNCP-accredited programs. The 1st year of the Bachelor lays the foundations of software development: basic algorithmics, front-end web development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript), object-oriented programming (Java, PHP), database management (SQL, MySQL), introduction to Linux systems and local networking. The pedagogy is project-driven from day one, with real team deliverables that simulate production conditions and professional workflows.

The Université Saint Jean in Yaoundé offers a preparatory class for engineering schools, equivalent to the French CPGE system. This intensive year covers mathematics (algebra, calculus, analytic geometry, trigonometry), physics (mechanics, electricity, thermodynamics, optics), chemistry, and an introduction to robotics and electronics. The goal is to develop analytical rigor and scientific reasoning to gain entry into engineering schools in Europe or Africa. The demanding pace and high standards of this preparatory class provided an essential academic foundation for the rest of the engineering journey.

projects.title: Stock Market Forecasting and Risk Management with GNNs
projects.description: Extension of the Differential Graph Transformer proposed in the Stanford CS224W article (https://medium.com/stanford-cs224w/stock-market-forecasting-with-differential-graph-transformer-62d095ebc821) into a predictive multi-task architecture. The model jointly forecasts asset returns and future volatility to drive a risk-aware portfolio allocation strategy. Includes a comprehensive ablation study on graph topologies (local, global, dual) and quantitative backtesting.

projects.title: Black-Scholes Modeling (Quant Finance)
projects.description: Numerical implementation of Stochastic Differential Equations (SDE) for asset pricing with dynamic drift/volatility calibration and Monte Carlo simulations (Euler-Maruyama).

projects.title: AI Job Tracker – Intelligent Personal ATS
projects.description: Full-stack automation system for job hunting built with Google Apps Script and Gemini 1.5 Flash. Developed an NLP pipeline to extract applications from Gmail, dynamic status tracking via sentiment analysis of recruiter replies, and an automated job sourcing engine using semantic scanning of newsletters.

projects.title: Traffic Accident Analysis & Prediction
projects.description: End-to-end Data Science pipeline on 7.7 million US road accidents (2016–2023) across 49 temporal, geospatial, meteorological and infrastructure features. Four simultaneous tasks: anomaly detection, severity prediction (levels 1–4) with Random Forest & XGBoost, geographic K-Means clustering, and analysis of road infrastructure influence (signals, junctions, crossings) on accident severity.

projects.title: TinyX Microservices Platform
projects.description: Full microservice architecture simulating a minimal social network in Java (Quarkus). Independent services communicating via REST APIs: user management (MongoDB), social graph (Neo4j), full-text search (Elasticsearch), caching and message queues (Redis). Orchestrated and deployed with Kubernetes. Each service is containerized and decoupled for independent scaling.

projects.title: Differentiable Programming Engine
projects.description: Rebuilding a deep learning framework similar to PyTorch to understand automatic differentiation and backpropagation.

projects.title: Galaxy Image Classification
projects.description: CNN trained on Galaxy10‑DECals dataset to evaluate convolutional model performance on astronomical data.

projects.title: CV Screening via NLP
projects.description: Automated resume screening using NLP pipelines, keyword extraction, and ML scoring.

projects.title: Recommendation Systems
projects.description: Collaborative filtering implementations (ItemKNN, UserKNN, SVD) with evaluation and diversification strategies.

projects.title: Medical Machine Learning
projects.description: Medical data classification, segmentation, DICOM parsing, and evaluation with Dice/Precision/Recall.

projects.title: Explainable AI (XAI)
projects.description: Evaluation of robustness and faithfulness of SHAP and LIME explanations; bias and uncertainty analysis.

projects.title: RL Trading Agent – MDP Trading Environment
projects.description: EPITA SCIA-G homework: modelling and solving a trading problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP). Implementation of a stochastic market environment (TradingEnvironment) in Python: discrete state space (price trend × position), three actions (Hold/Buy/Sell), shaped reward function (profit on sell, invalid action penalty −10, inaction penalty −0.1), finite-length episodes. Comparison of two RL approaches: value-based method (tabular Q-learning) and policy gradient method.

projects.title: Electromechanical Turing Machine
projects.description: Final-Year Project (PFE) carried out in partnership with EPITA. Design and fabrication of a fully functional physical Turing Machine. The project combines embedded electronics (Arduino, stepper motors, sensors), software implementation of a finite state machine (FSM) in C, and a real-time control interface. The machine physically reads and writes symbols on a tape, faithfully simulating the formal behavior of a Turing Machine. Supervised by EPITA faculty and researchers, delivered with full technical documentation.

projects.title: 42sh – POSIX Shell in C
projects.description: Flagship project of the C/Unix piscine at EPITA. Implemented a complete POSIX shell from scratch in pure C, matching bash --posix behavior. Built in a team of 5 over 4 weeks. Features implemented: full lexical analyzer (tokenization, quoting, variable expansion, tilde expansion), parser and AST construction, simple command execution, pipelines, redirections (stdin/stdout/stderr), compound commands, environment variables, and built-ins (echo, cd, export, unset, env, exit…). Zero memory leaks allowed, verified entirely with Valgrind. Built with CMake, tested with Criterion.

projects.title: Malloc – Memory Allocator in C
projects.description: Implementation from scratch of dynamic memory allocation functions: malloc, free, realloc and calloc, in pure C. Memory acquired from the OS via mmap. Block metadata management (headers, free lists), coalescing of adjacent free blocks to limit fragmentation, allocation strategies. Zero memory leaks verified with Valgrind. CMake build, EPITA test suite.

projects.title: Tiger – C++ Compiler (EPITA/LRDE)
projects.description: Full compiler built from scratch for the Tiger language (a statically typed language designed by EPITA's LRDE lab, based on Andrew Appel's textbooks). Complete 9-phase pipeline: lexical analysis (Flex), LALR parsing and AST construction (Bison), type checking and scope resolution, intermediate representation (IR trees), instruction selection via Maximal Munch (TC-7), liveness analysis (TC-8), register allocation (TC-9), ARM machine code generation. C++17, CMake, automated tests, Doxygen documentation.

projects.title: Ping – Clash of Clans Themed IDE
projects.description: Full-featured IDE developed in pair during the EPITA YAKA piscine. Java/Quarkus backend exposing a REST API for Maven project management: file/folder tree (FileNode/FolderNode), Maven compilation, indexed code search with Apache Lucene, full Git integration via JGit (commit, push, tree view). React frontend with a complete Clash of Clans theme: game-inspired animations, graphical design, and code editing features. Hexagonal architecture, JUnit 5 tests.

projects.title: Montreal Snow Removal Modeling
projects.description: Operational research and simulation project using Python.

projects.title: Medical AI – 3D Segmentation
projects.description: 3D segmentation of hepatic vessels and tumors on CT volumes. Complete PyTorch/MONAI pipeline including preprocessing, patch-based 3D UNet training, sliding-window inference, and Dice-based evaluation. Comparison with a classical ITK-based segmentation approach.

projects.title: Mini LeBonCoin – PHP Marketplace
projects.description: Simplified version of the French classifieds platform LeBonCoin built in PHP: user registration and login with session management, post and browse listings, image upload, responsive UI. MVC architecture, MySQL database, parameterized SQL queries to prevent injection attacks.

projects.title: React.js Portfolio
projects.description: Professional portfolio website built with React.js.

projects.title: Cinema Ticket Booking – Java Spring MVC
projects.description: Full-stack cinema ticket reservation web application: movie and showtime catalogue management, seat selection, real-time booking, user account with reservation history. Backend built with Java Spring MVC, MySQL database, Thymeleaf templating, responsive HTML/CSS frontend.

projects.title: Data Analytics Project
projects.description: Data processing, exploration, and visualization with Pandas and NumPy.

projects.title: Linux Web Server
projects.description: Linux web server setup, virtualization, and administration.

projects.title: Flask/PHP/Python Apps
projects.description: Web and object-oriented applications using Flask, PHP, and Django.

projects.title: Network Administration
projects.description: Network infrastructure setup and protocol management.

projects.title: Intelligent Parking System – Arduino
projects.description: Design and construction of a physical automated parking model during an internship at INNOVATECH (July–August 2019, Collège François-Xavier Vogt, Yaoundé). The system detects vehicles using HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensors and photoresistors, then automatically controls a servo-driven barrier. Two FSM (finite state machine) algorithms designed and implemented: vehicle presence detection and barrier open/close control. Hardware: Arduino MEGA, 3 HC-SR04 sensors, photoresistors, servomotor, breadboard. C programming with Arduino IDE.

projects.title: Gesture-Controlled Remote Car
projects.description: Group project (Institut Saint Jean, supervised by Dr. DJIOKO Jean Paul): design of a wireless remote-controlled car driven by hand movements. A glove fitted with an ADXL335 accelerometer captures tilt angles (X = forward/backward, Y = left/right, Z = up/down) and transmits commands at 2.4 GHz via NRF24L01 module. The car (Arduino Nano, L298N H-bridge, 4 gear motors, Li-ion battery) receives and executes commands in real time at over 50 m range. C code using SPI.h, nRF24L01.h, RF24.h libraries.

projects.title: DDPM – Pixel Art Generation via Diffusion
projects.description: Implementation of a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) for generating 32×32 pixel art sprites. The forward process progressively adds Gaussian noise over 1000 steps (Markov chain); the neural network learns to reverse this process to generate new coherent sprites. Trained on the Kaggle Pixel Art dataset with GPU acceleration (CUDA). Deliverables: GIF animations of the full diffusion process (noising and denoising), EMA stabilization metrics.

projects.title: LLM Society – Multi-Agent Simulation
projects.description: A multi-agent simulation framework exploring social dynamics using local LLMs. Each agent has a unique personality and psychological state parameters (opinion, valence, openness, trust, sociability) defined in YAML. Agents interact through structured JSON actions (messages, internal thoughts). Ollama backend runs models locally (phi, gemma3). Scenarios tested: secret number mission, Werewolf simulation. Each run generates a detailed timestamped log in outputs/.

projects.title: Link Prediction – RDF Knowledge Graph
projects.description: Complete Knowledge Graph Completion pipeline on an RDF graph of human relationships (human.ttl). Data loading and exploration with rdflib, negative triple generation, then training of four embedding models with PyKEEN: TransE, DistMult, ComplEx and RotatE. Evaluation by MR, MRR, Hits@1/3/10. Embedding visualization with PCA and t-SNE. Best model selection, top-k link generation and ontology consistency verification via OWLRL reasoning (constraint violation and disjoint class detection).

projects.title: Cybersecurity ML – Industrial Anomaly Detection
projects.description: Intrusion and anomaly detection on the SWaT (Secure Water Treatment) dataset — a real industrial water treatment system with sensors and actuators. Full pipeline: time series preprocessing, batch processing, normalization. Three unsupervised algorithms compared: Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM and Autoencoder. Evaluation: AUPRC, Balanced Accuracy, MCC (Matthews Correlation Coefficient). Supervised classification: SVM, Random Forest, neural network. Deliverables: notebook, 20-page report, 10-minute oral presentation.

projects.title: LEACH – Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN)
projects.description: Simulation and optimization of the LEACH protocol (Low-Energy Adaptive Clustering Hierarchy) for wireless sensor networks. Full energy model (E_ELEC, E_FS, E_MP, E_DA) across 100 sensors over 3000 communication rounds. Dynamic cluster head election via probabilistic threshold, topological optimization with K-Means. Event-driven simulation with SimPy. Interactive visualizations: residual energy, network lifetime, connectivity. Complementary coursework: distributed algorithms (logical clocks, consensus, mutual exclusion, global state).

projects.title: EPlace – Real-Time Collaborative Canvas
projects.description: Full-stack web application inspired by r/Place: a collaborative pixel canvas with real-time rooms and integrated chat. Vite.js frontend with Socket.io-client for real-time synchronization. Node.js/Express backend with WebSockets, JWT authentication, room management (canvas from 50×50 to 250×250 pixels), and JSON-configurable rate limiting. Monorepo client/server architecture with Docker, ESLint and Prettier.

projects.title: Stock Market Forecasting & Risk Management with GNN
projects.description: Applied research project: study and improvement of the Differential Graph Transformer (DGT) introduced in the Stanford CS224W article "Stock Market Forecasting with Differential Graph Transformer". The original model represents stocks as a dynamic graph (nodes = assets, edges = return correlations) and applies a differential transformer on graph embeddings to predict price movements. The project aims to analyze its limitations (temporal graph stability, sensitivity to market noise) and propose architectural or training improvements to enhance prediction accuracy and portfolio risk management.

projects.title: Neuro-Graph Trader – Autonomous Hedge Fund via GNN & RL
projects.description: Personal applied research project in quantitative finance: an autonomous portfolio management agent on S&P 500 stocks, combining Graph Neural Networks, deep reinforcement learning, and financial NLP. The market is modeled as a dynamic graph (nodes = assets, edges = Pearson correlations on sliding windows). Three multimodal streams are fused: OHLCV data (yfinance), graph embeddings (GAT then Dynamic Graph Transformer), and NLP signals (FinBERT – sentiment, informational stress index). The RL agent (PPO/SAC) learns in residual mode relative to Buy & Hold (Action_t = W_BH + α·π_θ), with an adaptive reward function based on market regime (risk-adjusted Sharpe, max drawdown, transaction costs). Strict walk-forward backtesting against Buy & Hold and Markowitz. XAI component: attention weight visualization and narrative explanation of decisions driven by NLP signals.
